purpose: Core compression component that compresses filtered air using oil cooling, with rotor wear degradation affecting sealing and compression efficiency

inputs.name: filtered_air_flow
inputs.description: Air flow rate entering the compressor element

inputs.name: cooled_oil_flow
inputs.description: Temperature of cooling oil entering the compressor element

outputs.name: system_pressure
outputs.description: Primary system pressure generated by compression

outputs.name: compressor_element_outlet_temperature
outputs.description: Temperature of compressed air exiting the element

outputs.name: compressed_air_oil_mixture
outputs.description: Pressure of compressed air-oil mixture before separation

states.name: rotor_clearances
states.description: Accumulated rotor clearance increase due to wear and thermal expansion

states.name: internal_sealing
states.description: Internal sealing effectiveness degrading due to rotor wear and clearance increase

states.name: oil_separator_blockage
states.description: Oil separator blockage level (0.0 = clean, 1.0 = fully blocked)

events.name: inlet_valve_stuck_closed
events.description: compressor starts running but does not load after delay time

parameters.name: sealing_degradation_timescale
parameters.description: Time constant for internal sealing degradation

parameters.name: base_compression_ratio
parameters.description: Baseline compression ratio at perfect sealing

parameters.name: temperature_rise_coefficient
parameters.description: Temperature rise per unit compression work

parameters.name: inlet_valve_stuck_threshold
parameters.description: Threshold for inlet valve stuck detection

state_transition_logic: rotor_clearances increases by clearance_wear_rate * filtered_air_flow * dt, clamped to [0.0, max_clearance]
internal_sealing decreases by (internal_sealing / sealing_degradation_timescale) * filtered_air_flow * dt, clamped to [0.0, 1.0]
inlet_valve_position approaches 1.0 using first-order lag with coefficient valve_response_coefficient, clamped to [0.0, 1.0]
oil_separator_blockage increases by separator_blockage_rate * dt, clamped to [0.0, 1.0]
output_logic: air_flow_output = filtered_air_flow * internal_sealing * inlet_valve_position * (1.0 - rotor_clearances / max_clearance * 0.3)
event_logic: inlet_valve_stuck_open occurs when inlet_valve_position > (1.0 - inlet_valve_stuck_threshold) and should be closed
assumptions: Rotor clearance increase is proportional to air flow rate and operating time
Internal sealing degradation follows exponential decay driven by air flow
Inlet valve operates normally under healthy conditions
Oil separator blockage accumulates gradually over time
Temperature rise is proportional to compression work
Rotor clearance affects air flow output efficiency
Oil separator blockage increases back-pressure in the system
